scope: Pass B full-surface expansion against the official v1.2 OpenAPI spec (github.com/cloudbeds/openapi-specs, src/pms-v1.2-openapi.yaml, 115 endpoints). Cloudbeds' separately-versioned Insights/Accounting/Guest-Profiles/Group-Profile/Payments-Vault/Fiscal-Document APIs are distinct products with their own OpenAPI specs and are out of scope for this v1.2 PMS connector.

excluded.reason: the response's "data" is an object keyed by property ID whose VALUES are themselves arrays of user objects (a keyed-array-of-arrays shape), and the endpoint's own OpenAPI schema does not even document the individual user object's fields (userID/name/email/role are undocumented); neither records.path (a single dotted path to one array) nor records.keyed_object (explodes a keyed object's OBJECT-typed values, not array-typed ones) can express this shape, and guessing an undocumented per-user field list risks silently wrong data

the endpoint's own OpenAPI schema documents "data" as {packageNames: string[]} — a bare array of package NAME strings, not record-shaped objects with an id/fields; connsdk.RecordsAt silently drops non-object array elements, so this endpoint cannot yield any record via the standard stream dialect
returns the identical package-names-only shape as getPackages' data.packageNames (see that entry's reason); a second endpoint name for the same non-record-shaped data
single-guest detail is a strict subset of the guests stream's own per-record shape already returned by GET /getGuestList
an alternate, narrower-filter view over the same guest records already returned by GET /getGuestList (the guests stream); no fields beyond what getGuestList exposes
an alternate, status-filtered view over the same guest records already returned by GET /getGuestList

an alternate, modified-date-filtered view over the same guest records already returned by GET /getGuestList (this bundle's incremental-read strategy, if any, would apply to the guests stream directly rather than a second parallel stream over identical data)
single-reservation detail is a strict subset of the reservations stream's own per-record shape already returned by GET /getReservations
an alternate view over the same reservation records already returned by GET /getReservations, adding rate-breakdown fields available via the separate rate_plans stream; not a distinct entity
a day-scoped housekeeping/front-desk room-assignment board snapshot (requires a specific date, not a stable catalog resource), narrower operational tooling than a syncable record stream

requires a subReservationID obtainable only from a getReservation/getReservationRoomDetails round-trip not otherwise modeled as a stream in this bundle; narrow per-room-stay drill-down, not a top-level catalog resource
an on-the-fly fee/tax quote calculator for a hypothetical stay (requires startDate/endDate/roomsTotal/adultsPerRoom as a booking-flow input), not a catalog of stored records
a live front-desk operational view (unassigned rooms for in-house/arriving reservations right now), not a stable catalog resource suitable for periodic sync

a physical-room-inventory list already covered by the rooms stream (GET /getRoomBlocks, this bundle's legacy-parity stream name) at the block level, and by room_types at the type level; the plain room list adds no entity this bundle does not already sync
a live shopping/availability-search endpoint for a hypothetical stay (requires startDate/endDate/rooms/adults as booking-flow input), not a stable catalog resource
a single-room-type rate lookup that returns the identical shape rate_plans already covers per-property; narrower scope, not a distinct entity
polls the async completion status of a bulk rate-update job (see patchRate/putRate below); job-status polling, not a data resource
per-app, per-property integration-partner settings storage; scoped to the calling app's own integration, not guest/property/reservation data
per-app (not per-property) integration-partner settings storage; scoped to the calling app's own integration
third-party integration-partner connect/disconnect state, documented as unavailable to ordinary property-level API clients
static property configuration (currency code/symbol/decimal formatting), not a syncable record entity
a pre-aggregated, date-scoped operational KPI summary (occupancy/ADR/RevPAR for one date) built for a UI dashboard widget, not a record-shaped catalog resource
guest-communication automation configuration (which templates fire on which triggers), not guest/reservation data
guest-communication template content configuration, not guest/reservation data

lists uploaded file attachments (documents/photos); the underlying files are binary downloads, and the file-upload counterparts (postFile/postGuestPhoto/postGuestDocument/postReservationDocument) are themselves excluded as binary_payload writes, so this metadata-only list has no corresponding write path in this bundle to be useful alongside

narrower per-group note listing; the group_notes write path (create_group_note) is covered, but the read side was deprioritized against the higher-value guest_notes/reservation_notes streams in this pass — a future capability-expansion pass can add a groups-fan-out read stream the same way guest_notes/reservation_notes were added here
a feature-flag/capability descriptor for which payment features this property's gateway integration supports, not guest/reservation/transaction data
OAuth2 authorization-server discovery metadata (issuer/endpoints), not property data
returns the authenticated user/token's own identity and granted property list; auth introspection, not property data
narrower per-allotment-block note listing; deprioritized against the higher-value allotment_blocks stream itself in this pass

the OAuth2/API-key token-exchange endpoint used to OBTAIN the api_key credential this bundle's spec.json already requires; a credential-acquisition flow, not a data mutation, and out of the declarative auth dialect's scope (this bundle's auth.mode: bearer assumes a token already provisioned via this endpoint outside the connector)

a compound guest-to-room assignment action (add/remove multiple guest ids against a room in one call, with removeAll/mainGuestId semantics); the simpler per-guest create_guest/update_guest and per-room assign_room writes already cover the common single-guest cases without the compound multi-id request shape
attaches a binary file upload (form-based file field) to a reservation
attaches a binary file upload (form-based file field) to a guest
uploads a binary photo file for a guest
generic binary file upload, optionally attached to a group profile

a hosted-payment-page charge flow returning a redirectUrl for the guest to complete payment out-of-band; a multi-step, redirect-driven checkout flow rather than a single declarative request/response mutation like post_payment
stores a raw credit card number/PAN server-side; out of PCI scope for a declarative connector to ever transmit or persist, regardless of transport encryption
creates a new custom payment-method TYPE for the property's payment configuration (an admin/settings action), not a transactional payment record
generates a jurisdiction-specific fiscal/government receipt document; a specialized compliance document-generation flow, not a general folio mutation

an append-mode variant of postCustomItem with an identical request/response shape (adds items to an existing custom sale rather than creating a new one); postCustomItem itself is out of scope (see below), so its append variant is too

posts an ad-hoc, non-catalog custom line item with an embedded payments array (a compound sale+payment write); the catalog-backed post_item (references a real itemID from the items stream) covers the common, cataloged case with a simpler, single-purpose request shape

bulk-creates a new stock item definition (item master data), overlapping create_item_category's item-catalog-management scope but for stock/inventory items specifically; deprioritized against the higher-value transactional post_item/void_item writes in this pass
updates stock item master data (name/SKU/price/reorder threshold); item-catalog management, deprioritized against transactional item writes in this pass (see postItemsToInventory)
defines a new custom-field SCHEMA property for the property's guest/reservation forms (an admin/settings action), not a per-record data write

an alternate route to the same group-update operation as putGroup (Cloudbeds documents both a POST /patchGroup and a POST /putGroup for group updates); putGroup is the canonical one this bundle would cover if group writes were in scope, and full group CRUD was deprioritized in this pass against the higher-value guest/reservation/payment/housekeeping writes

full group-profile update (address/tax/commission/legal-name fields); deprioritized in this pass — create_group_note covers the common note-adding case, but broader group-profile CRUD was not implemented
guest-communication automation configuration; not guest/reservation/transaction data
guest-communication template content configuration; not guest/reservation/transaction data
submits an integration-partner error report to Cloudbeds' own MFD support tooling; app-diagnostics channel, not property data
writes per-app, per-property integration-partner settings; scoped to the calling app's own integration configuration
deletes per-app, per-property integration-partner settings; scoped to the calling app's own integration configuration
updates per-app, per-property integration-partner settings; scoped to the calling app's own integration configuration
third-party integration-partner connect/disconnect state management, documented as unavailable to ordinary property-level API clients
a single-property detail lookup that is a strict subset of the hotels stream's own per-record shape already returned by GET /getHotels
single-item detail is a strict subset of the items stream's own per-record shape already returned by GET /getItems

bulk rate-plan/pricing update, processed asynchronously via getRateJobs; a specialized revenue-management bulk-write flow (large JSON rate-grid payloads) outside this connector's per-record CRUD scope
an alternate route to the same bulk rate-update operation as putRate (see that entry's reason)

allotment-block CREATE was deprioritized against the read-side allotment_blocks stream in this pass; the block-management write surface (create/update/delete/notes) is a cohesive group left for a future capability-expansion pass rather than partially implemented
see createAllotmentBlock's reason; part of the same deprioritized allotment-block write group
see createAllotmentBlock's reason; part of the same deprioritized allotment-block write group
see createAllotmentBlock's reason; part of the same deprioritized allotment-block write group
see createAllotmentBlock's reason; part of the same deprioritized allotment-block write group
